title: 18_100754_ General 1946-7_Vol_2
object_description: Flying discs/saucers, with no further physical details specified in the record description.
summary_one_line: Memorandums and correspondence within the Department of War document Air Materiel Command concerns regarding flying disc and saucer sightings in 1946–1947.

title: 18_6369445_General_1948_Vol_1
object_description: Flying discs as referenced in administrative correspondence; no specific physical description provided in the record metadata.
summary_one_line: Department of War file containing memorandums and correspondence related to flying disc sightings and investigations from mid-1948.

title: 255_413270_UFO's_and_Defense_What_Should_we_Prepare_For
summary_one_line: A declassified NASA file contains the French COMETA report on UFOs and defense preparedness, originally published in 1999, along with a letter from Carol Rosin regarding Wernher von Braun.

title: NASA-UAP-D3, Gemini 7 Transcript, 1965
object_description: A 'bogey' (unknown craft) accompanied by a large debris field of 'very, very many hundreds of little particles' estimated four miles from the spacecraft; Lovell also observed a 'brilliant body in the sun against a black background with trillions of particles on it.'
summary_one_line: Gemini 7 astronauts Borman and Lovell reported observing an unknown 'bogey' and a debris field of hundreds of particles in low Earth orbit on December 5, 1965.

title: NASA-UAP-D3A, Gemini 7 Audio Excerpt, 1965
object_description: Described verbally by Astronaut Frank Borman as a 'bogey'; no detailed physical shape, color, or size description is provided in the available record beyond it being an unidentified object observed from low Earth orbit.
summary_one_line: During the Gemini 7 mission on December 5, 1965, astronaut Frank Borman reported sighting an unidentified object, referred to as a 'bogey,' from low Earth orbit, corroborated by fellow crew member Jim Lovell.

title: 331_120752_Numeric_Files_1944–1945_37153_German_Armament_Equipment_Documents
object_description: Night phenomena ('foofighters'), unidentified cylindrical objects, blinking lights, and flak rockets; behavior and appearance varied across multiple observations including glowing or blinking aerial phenomena.
summary_one_line: SHAEF documents from March 1945 record multiple observations by the 415th Night Fighter Squadron of 'foofighters,' unidentified cylindrical objects, flak rockets, and blinking lights over Germany.

title: 341_110448_Records_Relating_to_the_Collection_and_Dissemination_of_Intelligence_1948-1955-TS_CONT_No.2_2-5300-2-5399
object_description: Unidentified flying objects described as flying saucers; specific shape, color, and behavior details not available in the record blurb.
summary_one_line: An Air Force intelligence report from November 1948 documents observations of unidentified flying objects and flying saucers over the Netherlands.

title: 341_110677_Numerical_File,_5-2500
object_description: Unconventional aircraft observed during ascent and flight; specific shape, color, and physical details not described in the available record excerpt.
summary_one_line: An eyewitness reported observing the ascent and flight of an unconventional aircraft in the trans-Caucasus region of the USSR on 14 October 1955.

title: 342_HS1-416511228_319.1 Flying Discs 1949
object_description: Flying discs of unspecified shape and color, exhibiting varied movement and altitude characteristics as described in individual incident reports; some reports include diagrams of appearance.
summary_one_line: Military and civilian aviation personnel reported multiple UFO incidents in compliance with FSR 200-4, with sightings documented across various locations including Japan.

title: 38_143685_box_Incident_Summaries_101-172
summary_one_line: Collection of UAP incident summaries (cases 101–172) compiled by the Department of War, each containing checklist details, witness statements, and narrative reports.

title: 38_143685_box_Incident_Summaries_173-233
summary_one_line: A collection of UFO incident summaries (cases 173–233) compiled by the Department of War, each containing checklist details, witness statements, and narrative descriptions of unidentified flying object sightings.

title: 38_143685_box7_Incident_Summaries_1-100
summary_one_line: A collection of 1-100 UAP incident summaries compiled by the Department of War, each containing checklist details, witness statements, and narrative descriptions of unidentified flying object encounters.

title: 59_214434_SP 16 [7.18.1963]
summary_one_line: A 1963 Executive Office memorandum from the National Aeronautics and Space Council discusses policy plans regarding potential discovery of alien intelligence, life on Mars, and diplomatic considerations.
A 1952 Department of State memorandum discusses increased UFO sighting reports, offering possible explanations including technological improvements, historical records, and U.S. Air Force opinions.

title: 65_HS1-101634279_100-DE-18221_Serial_844
object_description: Circular object with a crystal-type dome
summary_one_line: A Detroit man reported observing a circular UFO with a crystal-type dome to the FBI in April 1958, prompting a recommendation to forward the report to Air Force authorities.

title: 65_HS1-101634279_100-DE-26505
object_description: Large, circular vehicle capable of vertical ascent, observed near a German military compound in 1944 Germany.
summary_one_line: In 1957, FBI agents interviewed Wladyslaw Krasuski, who reported witnessing a large circular craft rising vertically near a German military compound in 1944.

title: 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_8

title: DOW-UAP-D10, Mission Report, Middle East, May 2022
object_description: Five objects observed crossing a sensor screen; one described as a possible missile, four described as possible birds; no further shape or color details provided.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator reported observing five UAP cross the screen during a mission over Iraq in May 2022, with one assessed as a possible missile and four as possible birds.

title: DOW-UAP-D12, Mission Report, Iraq, May 2022
object_description: Single unidentified aerial object flying in a north to northeast direction; no further physical description available due to redaction.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator in Iraq observed and tracked a single UAP traveling north to northeast but was unable to positively identify it.

title: DOW-UAP-D14, Mission Report, Iraq, May 2022
object_description: Single UAP observed flying north to northeast; no further physical description available due to redaction.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed and followed a single UAP traveling north to northeast over Syria in May 2022, but was unable to positively identify it.

title: DOW-UAP-D16, Mission Report, Syria, July 2022
object_description: One unidentified aerial phenomenon observed moving from north to south; duration of observation was under one minute; no further physical description available due to redaction.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed a single UAP traveling north to south over Syria for under one minute on July 31, 2022.

title: DOW-UAP-D18, Mission Report, Iraq, December 2022
object_description: One unidentified object flying from west to east; no further physical description provided.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator in Iraq reported observing one possible UAP traveling from west to east, which was not pursued.

title: DOW-UAP-D19, Mission Report, Syria, February 21, 2023
object_description: Possible balloon observed at approximately 2,100 feet altitude; no further shape, color, or behavioral details provided.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator in Syria reported observing one possible balloon at approximately 2,100 feet altitude on February 21, 2023.

title: DOW-UAP-D20, Mission Report, Iraq, 2023
object_description: Several bright objects maneuvering quickly from west to east northeast; tracked via onboard targeting pod for ~20 seconds before dimming and disappearing.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed several bright, fast-moving objects traveling west to east northeast over Iraq, tracking them for approximately 20 seconds via an onboard targeting pod before they dimmed and disappeared.

title: DOW-UAP-D23, Mission Report, United Arab Emirates, October 2023
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator reported observing one UAP over the Arabian Gulf on October 31, 2023, documented via a standardized Mission Report submitted to AARO.

title: DOW-UAP-D23, Mission Report, United Arab Emirates, October 2023
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator reported observing one UAP over the Arabian Gulf region near the United Arab Emirates on October 31, 2023.

title: DOW-UAP-D25, Mission Report, Greece, January 2024
object_description: Diamond-shaped object with a non-maneuvering probe at the bottom, traveling at approximately 434 knots (499 mph), only visible through Short-Wave Infrared (SWIR) sensor imagery.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed a diamond-shaped UAP traveling at approximately 434 knots over the Mediterranean Sea, visible only via SWIR sensor, for roughly two minutes.

title: DOW-UAP-D27, Mission Report, United Arab Emirates, October 2023
object_description: Single unidentified aerial phenomenon observed at approximately 24,000 feet altitude, estimated speed of 163 knots (187 mph); no further physical description provided due to redaction.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed a single UAP at approximately 24,000 feet altitude traveling at an estimated 163 knots over the Gulf of Oman in October 2023.

title: DOW-UAP-D28, Mission Report, Iraq, September 2024
object_description: Lens flare observed through MX-20 and MX-25 infrared sensors, described as moving through the sensor's field-of-view at a high rate of speed and associated with a significant heat source; no definitive physical shape or color described.
summary_one_line: During a weapons calibration test over Iraq, U.S. military operators observed a fast-moving lens flare associated with a significant heat source via IR sensors after firing an AGM-176 Griffin missile.

title: DOW-UAP-D3, Mission Report, Arabian Gulf, 2020
object_description: A formation described as a 'line of dots followed by a trailing dot,' suggesting multiple objects or lights arranged in a linear pattern with one lagging behind.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator in the Arabian Gulf reported observing a line of dots followed by a trailing dot during a 2020 mission.

title: DOW-UAP-D32, Mission Report, Syria, October 2024
object_description: Misshapen and uneven ball of white light with multiple glares or light emanating from an unknown origin, described as a light/glare halo effect visible at the top of a Full-Motion Video feed.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator in Syria observed a misshapen ball of white light with multiple glares emanating from an unknown origin, appearing as a light/glare halo effect on a Full-Motion Video feed.

title: DOW-UAP-D32, Mission Report, Syria, October 2024
object_description: Misshapen and uneven ball of white light with multiple glares or light emanating from an unknown origin, described as a light/glare halo effect observed at the top of a Full-Motion Video feed.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed a misshapen ball of white light with multiple glares of unknown origin appearing as a halo effect at the top of a Full-Motion Video feed over Syria in October 2024.

title: DOW-UAP-D32, Mission Report, Syria, October 2024
object_description: Misshapen and uneven ball of white light with multiple glares or light emanating from an unknown origin, described as a light/glare halo effect observed at the top of the FMV feed.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed a misshapen ball of white light with multiple glares forming a halo effect at the top of a Full-Motion Video feed over Syria in October 2024.

title: DOW-UAP-D33, Mission Report, Greece, October 2023
object_description: Unidentified object observed flying just above the ocean surface, performing multiple 90-degree turns at an estimated speed of 80 mph; no specific shape or color recorded.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed a UAP flying just above the ocean surface over the Aegean Sea, executing multiple sharp 90-degree turns at approximately 80 mph.

title: DOW-UAP-D35, Mission Report, Greece, October 2023
object_description: UAP observed flying just above the surface of the ocean, traveling in a straight path toward land; no shape or color details provided.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed a UAP flying just above the ocean surface and moving straight toward land over the Aegean Sea on October 29, 2023.

title: DOW-UAP-D38, Range Fouler Debrief, Middle East, May 2020
object_description: Solid white object that flew through the field-of-view and made erratic movements above the water surface.
summary_one_line: A U.S. Navy operator observed a solid white object making erratic movements above the water in the Arabian Gulf in May 2020.

title: DOW-UAP-D4, Mission Report, Arabian Gulf, 2020
object_description: Unidentified aerial object observed traveling at an estimated speed of 321 knots (369 mph); reportedly increased speed and changed direction toward the east; no physical shape or color described.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator in the Arabian Gulf observed a UAP traveling at approximately 321 knots that increased speed and changed direction toward the east.

title: DOW-UAP-D42, Range Fouler Debrief, Japan, 2023
object_description: Three unidentified aerial objects observed moving amongst each other; one object flew through the screen while a second surpassed the first at higher speed; no specific shape or color described.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator reported observing three UAP moving amongst each other, with one object surpassing another at higher speed, during a range fouler incident over the Arabian Gulf on August 31, 2020.

title: DOW-UAP-D44, Range Fouler Reporting Form, Gulf of Aden, October 2020
object_description: Round, cold object detected via infrared sensor, traveling at 319 degrees (northwest) at approximately 20 mph, exhibiting abrupt directional changes.
summary_one_line: A U.S. Navy operator detected a round, cold object via infrared sensor over the Arabian Sea making abrupt directional changes while traveling northwest at approximately 20 mph.

title: DOW-UAP-D48, Department of the Air Force Report, 1996
summary_one_line: A 1996 Air Force report documents modeling of unlikely space-booster failure modes in risk calculations, recommending corrective actions via novel techniques.

title: DOW-UAP-D49, Launch Summary, Vandenberg AFB, 2000
summary_one_line: Historical summary report documenting launches at Vandenberg Air Force Base between 1958 and 2000, released as part of UAP records.

title: DOW-UAP-D5, Mission Report, Arabian Gulf, 2020
object_description: Two unidentified aerial phenomena traveling at an estimated speed of 278 knots (320 mph), observed to increase speed and change direction toward the south; no shape or color specified.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed two UAP traveling at approximately 278 knots that increased speed and changed direction toward the south over the Arabian Gulf in 2020.

title: DOW-UAP-D50, Email Correspondence, INDOPACOM, April 2025
summary_one_line: Email correspondence within INDOPACOM requests clarification on the contents of a UAP mission report from April 2025.

title: DOW-UAP-D51, Email Correspondence, Pacific Time Zone, March 2023
summary_one_line: Email correspondence requesting clarification on a UAP mission report from the Pacific Time Zone in March 2023.

title: DOW-UAP-D52, Email Correspondance, NA, August 2024
summary_one_line: Email correspondence requesting clarification on the contents of a UAP mission report from August 2024.

title: DOW-UAP-D54, Mission Report, Mediterranean Sea, NA
object_description: Triangular and metallic UAP observed at 24,989 feet altitude and 168 knots (193 mph) speed.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator over the Mediterranean Sea observed a single triangular, metallic UAP at approximately 24,989 feet altitude traveling at 168 knots.

title: DOW-UAP-D55, Mission Report, Syria, November 2016
object_description: Object observed via EO/IR sensor appearing to operate in 'sea skim mode' (very low altitude), traveling at approximately 500 knots (575 mph) on a southeasterly heading; no shape or color details recorded.
summary_one_line: A U.S. Navy P-8A pilot observed an unidentified object via EO/IR sensor traveling at approximately 500 knots in 'sea skim mode' near Latakia, Syria, losing contact after two minutes.

title: DOW-UAP-D56, Range Fouler Debrief, Arabian Sea, August 2020
object_description: Three small unidentified air contacts with discernible wings/airframe structure; initially observed on a westerly heading, with two additional objects appearing to the east of the first after it briefly passed behind a cloud; all three maintained relative course, speed, and altitude.
summary_one_line: A U.S. Navy operator reported tracking three small unidentified aerial contacts with visible wing/airframe structure over the North Arabian Sea that maintained consistent course, speed, and altitude.

title: DOW-UAP-D57, Range Fouler Reporting Form, Gulf of Aden, September 2020
object_description: Round, cold object appearing bright white under black-hot IR sensor; traveled at 277 mph on a heading of 168 degrees and made several abrupt directional changes.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator tracked a round, cold object via IR sensor over the Gulf of Aden for eight minutes as it traveled at 277 mph and made abrupt directional changes.

title: DOW-UAP-D58, Range Fouler Debrief, NA, October 2020
object_description: Balloon-shaped, metallic, and reflective objects described as two red blinking strobes; one was observed circling the other.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator encountered two balloon-shaped, metallic, reflective UAP with red blinking strobes, one of which was circling the other, during an unauthorized airspace intrusion.

title: DOW-UAP-D6, Mission Report, Arabian Gulf, 2020
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator reported observing an unidentified aerial phenomenon over the Arabian Gulf in 2020, documented in a standardized military mission report.

title: DOW-UAP-D60, Mission Report, Arabian Gulf, August 2020
object_description: Single UAP observed in transit; no detailed physical description provided due to intermittent cloud coverage affecting FMV sensor collection.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator in the Arabian Gulf reported observing a single transiting UAP with no mission impact, though dense cloud coverage intermittently hindered full-motion video collection.

title: DOW-UAP-D61, Mission Report, Arabian Gulf, August 2020
object_description: Formation of unknown flying objects; continuous tracking was hindered by light cloud coverage; traveling northeast to northwest along the coast.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed a formation of unknown flying objects traveling northeast to northwest along the Arabian Gulf coast for approximately two minutes in August 2020.

title: DOW-UAP-D62, Mission Report, Strait of Hormuz, September 2020
object_description: One UAP observed at an estimated altitude of 1,800 feet; no further physical description available due to document redaction.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator reported observing a single UAP at an estimated altitude of 1,800 feet over the Strait of Hormuz on September 16, 2020.

title: DOW-UAP-D63, Mission Report, Strait of Hormuz, October 2020
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator reported observing an unidentified aerial phenomenon over the Strait of Hormuz in October 2020.

title: DOW-UAP-D64, Mission Report, Iran, November 2020
object_description: Two separate UAP observed at unknown altitude; second object observed traveling in a northwest direction; no further physical description available due to redaction.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator reported observing two UAP over Iran on November 2, 2020, at 2143Z and 2148Z, with the second traveling northwest.

title: DOW-UAP-D65, Mission Report, Arabian Gulf, July 2020
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator reported three separate UAP encounters over the Arabian Gulf on July 16, 2020, at 1830Z, 1920Z, and 2345Z.

title: DOW-UAP-D7, Mission Report, Arabian Gulf, 2020
object_description: Balloon-like object traveling with the winds at approximately 31,000 ft altitude, visually tracked via onboard infrared sensor.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed a balloon-like UAP traveling with the winds at approximately 31,000 ft over the Arabian Gulf, tracked via onboard infrared sensor.

title: DOW-UAP-D74, Mission Report, Syria, November 2023
object_description: Spherical object described as 'shaped as a bouncy ball,' traveling at approximately 424 knots (483 mph) consistently for at least 7 minutes, approaching from the south; assessed as benign by the operator.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator in Syria observed a spherical UAP traveling at approximately 424 knots consistently for at least 7 minutes, approaching from the south.

title: DOW-UAP-D75, Mission Report, Gulf of Aden, July 2024
object_description: Single UAP maintaining a straight flight path at constant altitude; speed assessed as faster than normal flying speed; operator assessed the object as benign; no shape or color details provided due to redaction.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator observed a single UAP over the Gulf of Aden on July 14, 2024, traveling in a straight flight path at an altitude-consistent speed faster than conventional flying speed before moving out of visual range.

title: DOW-UAP-D8, Mission Report, Djibouti, 2025
object_description: Two 'white hot' UAPs observed visually, estimated speed approximately 240 nautical miles per hour (276 mph); no further shape or size details provided.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military operator reported observing two white hot UAPs traveling at approximately 240 knots over the Mediterranean Sea near Djibouti in 2025.

title: DOW-UAP-PR19, Unresolved UAP Report, Middle East, May 2022
object_description: An area of contrast moving from left to right across the bottom third of the infrared sensor field-of-view at the two-second mark; mission report characterized it as a 'possible missile,' with four other unseen objects described as 'possible birds.'
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor over the Middle East captured five seconds of footage in May 2022 showing an unidentified object moving across the field-of-view, tentatively described as a possible missile.

title: DOW-UAP-PR20, Unresolved UAP Report, Kuwait, May 2022
object_description: Elongated area of contrast increasing in intensity along its length from top left to bottom right, moving from north to northeast; exact shape, color, and size undetermined from available imagery.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military system captured a still image of an unidentified elongated aerial phenomenon moving north to northeast over Kuwait/Iraq in May 2022, which operators could not positively identify.

title: DOW-UAP-PR21, Unresolved UAP Report, Iraq, May 2022
object_description: Two areas of contrast moving together near the center of the infrared sensor's field-of-view, tentatively identified in the mission report as a probable SU-27 or SU-35 Russian military aircraft.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor recorded ten seconds of video over Iraq in May 2022 depicting an unidentified object tentatively described as a probable Russian SU-27 or SU-35 aircraft.

title: DOW-UAP-PR22, Unresolved UAP Report, Syria, July 2022
object_description: An unidentified object observed moving from right to left (north to south) across the top right quarter of the sensor field-of-view, captured on both infrared and electro-optical sensors; no further physical shape or color details available due to redaction.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared and electro-optical sensor captured 14 seconds of video footage over Syria in July 2022 showing an unidentified object moving from north to south across the sensor field-of-view.

title: DOW-UAP-PR23, Unresolved UAP Report, Iraq, December 2022
object_description: An area of contrast observed on infrared sensor footage, moving from the bottom left to the top right of the field-of-view before exiting the frame at approximately six seconds; described in the mission report as flying west to east.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor captured ten seconds of video showing an unidentified aerial phenomenon moving west to east over Iraq in December 2022.

title: DOW-UAP-PR26, Unresolved UAP Report, United Arab Emirates, October 2023
object_description: An area of contrast detected by an infrared sensor, remaining generally within the top left quarter of the display before passing through center during sensor panning; no definitive shape, color, or physical characteristics determinable from the infrared video alone.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor aboard a platform operating near the United Arab Emirates captured 43 seconds of unidentified aerial phenomenon footage in October 2023, submitted to AARO as an unresolved case.

title: DOW-UAP-PR27, Unresolved UAP Report, United Arab Emirates, October 2023
object_description: An area of contrast detected by an infrared (IR) sensor; no distinct shape, color, or physical features resolved. The object remained generally centered in the sensor field-of-view before erratic sensor motion caused it to move across the display, with the system repeatedly losing and reacquiring it.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor aboard a CENTCOM platform captured approximately five minutes of video of an unidentified area of contrast over the United Arab Emirates in October 2023.

title: DOW-UAP-PR28, Unresolved UAP Report, Greece, January 2024
object_description: Diamond-shaped object moving at approximately 434 knots, visible only via short-wave infrared (SWIR) sensor; visually resembled an inverted teardrop with a vertically linear trailing mass suspended below; not detectable in electro-optical or visible spectrum imagery.
summary_one_line: A diamond-shaped UAP traveling at approximately 434 knots was detected solely via SWIR sensor by a U.S. military platform over Greece in January 2024.

title: DOW-UAP-PR29, Unresolved UAP Report, United Arab Emirates, June 2024
object_description: An inverted teardrop-shaped object with a vertical pole or bar attached to the bottom; observed via infrared sensor; remained generally centered in the field of view; possibly a reflection from an object in the water.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor captured 21 seconds of video footage of an unidentified object resembling an inverted teardrop with a vertical bar or pole suspended below it over the Gulf of Oman in June 2024.

title: DOW-UAP-PR31, Unresolved UAP Report, Syria, October 2024
object_description: A misshapen and uneven ball of white light exhibiting a light/glare halo effect at the top of the FMV feed; appears as an indistinctly shaped multi-colored area moving from right to left across the top edge of the sensor display within the first second of video.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military FMV camera over Syria captured five seconds of video showing a misshapen ball of white light with a halo effect moving across the top edge of the sensor display in October 2024.

title: DOW-UAP-PR32, Unresolved UAP Report, Syria, October 2024
object_description: A misshapen and uneven ball of white light exhibiting a light/glare halo effect; in video, appears as an irregularly shaped area of white and red highlights forming a horizontally-oriented half-oval spanning roughly one-third of the frame width near the top edge of the sensor display.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military FMV camera over Syria captured six seconds of video showing an unidentified misshapen ball of white light with a halo effect in October 2024.

title: DOW-UAP-PR33, Unresolved UAP Report, Syria, October 2024
object_description: A misshapen and uneven ball of white light with a light/glare halo effect at the top of the FMV feed; video shows two semi-transparent, irregularly shaped orange areas overlaying the background imagery for less than two seconds each.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military FMV camera over Syria captured five seconds of footage showing a misshapen ball of white light with a halo effect, submitted to AARO as an unresolved UAP.

title: DOW-UAP-PR34, Unresolved UAP Report, Greece, October 2023
object_description: An area of infrared contrast observed flying near the ocean surface, executing multiple 90-degree turns at approximately 80 miles per hour; no distinct shape or color discernible beyond a trackable contrast signature on infrared sensor footage.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor tracked an unidentified aerial phenomenon near the ocean surface off Greece in October 2023, observing it make sharp 90-degree turns at approximately 80 mph before losing lock.

title: DOW-UAP-PR35, Unresolved UAP Report, Greece, October 2023
object_description: Small and circular object flying near the surface of the ocean toward land, detected as an area of contrast on an infrared sensor; became indistinguishable as background transitioned from water to land.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor captured 24 seconds of video of a small, circular UAP flying low over the ocean toward land near Greece in October 2023.

title: DOW-UAP-PR36, Unresolved UAP Report, Middle East, May 2020
object_description: Solid white object making erratic movements above water, observed as an area of contrast in infrared sensor footage; a blue reticle attempted but failed to acquire a lock on the object.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor captured 2 minutes and 17 seconds of footage of an unidentified solid white object making erratic movements above water in the Middle East in May 2020.

title: DOW-UAP-PR37, Unresolved UAP Report, Middle East, 2020
object_description: An area of contrast detected by infrared sensor, entering from the bottom left of the frame and traveling in a generally linear path upward, exiting from the top left; no shape, color, or physical details determinable from available footage.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor over the Arabian Gulf captured nine seconds of video in 2020 showing an unidentified object traversing the field-of-view in a linear path, submitted to AARO as an unresolved UAP.

title: DOW-UAP-PR38, Unresolved UAP Report, Middle East, 2013
object_description: An area of contrast resembling an eight-pointed star with arms of alternating length, observed via infrared sensor, exhibiting movement within the field-of-view and leaving a visible trail before exiting the frame.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor captured 1 minute 46 seconds of video footage of an unidentified eight-pointed star-shaped area of contrast with a visible trail over the Middle East in 2013.

title: DOW-UAP-PR39, Unresolved UAP Report, Middle East, 2020
object_description: A faint area of contrast detected by infrared sensor, moving from the right edge to the bottom center of the frame over approximately two seconds; no shape, size, or color determinable from available footage.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor aboard a CENTCOM platform captured five seconds of footage showing an unidentified object traversing the frame over the Arabian Gulf in 2020.

title: DOW-UAP-PR40, Unresolved UAP Report, Middle East, 2020
object_description: Small thermal signature detected by infrared sensor; appears as an area of contrast brightening against the background, tracked via sensor pan across multiple contrast and zoom settings; no defined shape or color beyond thermal anomaly.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor aboard a platform over the Arabian Gulf captured 63 seconds of video showing an unidentified small thermal signature tracked across the sensor field-of-view in 2020.

title: DOW-UAP-PR41, Unresolved UAP Report, Middle East, 2020
object_description: An area of contrast detected by an infrared sensor; no shape, color, or physical characteristics described beyond its appearance as a trackable contrast region within the sensor field-of-view.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor aboard a Central Command platform tracked an unidentified area of contrast over the Arabian Gulf for approximately 94 seconds in 2020.

title: DOW-UAP-PR42, Unresolved UAP Report, Middle East, 2020
object_description: An unidentified area of contrast detected via infrared sensor, exhibiting intermittent loss of distinctiveness against the background, apparent disappearance and reappearance, and movement across the sensor field-of-view; no shape, color, or physical form could be determined from the footage.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor aboard a CENTCOM platform recorded nearly five minutes of video tracking an unidentified area of contrast over the Arabian Gulf in 2020.

title: DOW-UAP-PR43, Unresolved UAP Report, Africa, 2025
object_description: Small, barely distinguishable area of contrast moving from the left side to the bottom-right of the infrared sensor field-of-view; no shape, color, or additional physical detail discernible.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor over Djibouti captured two seconds of video showing an unidentified small object traversing the field-of-view from left to bottom-right.

title: DOW-UAP-PR44, Unresolved UAP Report, Middle East, 2020
object_description: An area of contrast detected by an infrared sensor; no shape, color, or physical form was described. The object was tracked for roughly three minutes, cycled through various zoom and reticle levels, and exited the frame from the top left quadrant when tracking ceased.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor aboard a Central Command platform tracked an unidentified area of contrast for approximately five minutes over the Arabian Gulf in 2020.

title: DOW-UAP-PR45, Unresolved UAP Report, Middle East, 2020
object_description: An unidentified area of contrast detected via infrared sensor; gradually increased in size and distinctiveness as the platform closed distance, then rapidly exited the frame through the bottom right corner; no shape, color, or physical form confirmed.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor detected and tracked an unidentified area of contrast for 58 seconds over the Southern United States in 2020 before it exited the sensor's field of view.

title: DOW-UAP-PR46, Unresolved UAP Report, INDOPACOM, 2024
object_description: Football-shaped body with three radial projections: one oriented vertically and two oriented downward at approximately 45-degree angles relative to the object's major axis, observed via infrared sensor.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor aboard an INDOPACOM platform captured nine seconds of video showing an unidentified football-shaped object with three radial projections over the East China Sea in 2024.

title: DOW-UAP-PR47, Unresolved UAP Report, INDOPACOM, 2023
object_description: Three distinct areas of contrast observed via infrared sensor, maintaining fixed positions and orientations relative to one another and generally centered within the frame throughout the footage.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor captured approximately two minutes of video showing three unidentified areas of contrast maintaining fixed relative positions over Japan in 2023.

title: DOW-UAP-PR48, Unresolved UAP Report, INDOPACOM, 2024
object_description: An area of contrast detected by an infrared sensor, maintaining a generally centered position within the frame throughout the 99-second recording; no shape, color, or physical characteristics were described.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor aboard a platform in the Indo-Pacific region captured 99 seconds of video tracking an unidentified area of contrast with no accompanying verbal or written description from the reporter.

title: DOW-UAP-PR49, Unresolved UAP Report, Department of the Army, 2026
object_description: Two areas of contrast detected by an infrared sensor aboard a U.S. military platform; no physical shape, color, or oral/written description was provided by the reporter; objects maintained generally centered positions while the sensor zoomed and panned.
summary_one_line: A U.S. military infrared sensor recorded 1 minute 49 seconds of video tracking two unidentified areas of contrast in an unresolved UAP report submitted by the Department of the Army to AARO in 2026.

title: FBI Photo A1
object_description: Small, dark, and slightly irregular object visible in a monochrome, grainy image just below and to the right of a central crosshair reticle; no further shape, size, or behavioral details available due to redactions.
summary_one_line: The FBI submitted a redacted still image from a U.S. government system to AARO showing a small, dark, irregular object near a crosshair reticle that operators could not positively identify.

title: FBI Photo A2
object_description: Dark, circular object centered within a crosshair reticle, observed in a monochrome image against a mottled, textured background suggesting a varied landscape or surface.
summary_one_line: The FBI submitted a redacted still image of a dark, circular UAP captured by a U.S. government system in late 2025, with date and location withheld.

title: FBI Photo A3
object_description: Dark, circular object centered precisely within a crosshair reticle, observed in a monochrome image against a mottled, textured background possibly depicting ground terrain; no size, speed, or behavioral data provided.
summary_one_line: The FBI submitted a redacted still image of an unidentified dark circular object centered in a crosshair reticle, captured by a U.S. government system in late 2025, with date and location withheld.

title: FBI Photo A4
object_description: Dark, circular object visible just below and to the right of the center of a crosshair reticle in a monochrome image with a mottled background.
summary_one_line: FBI submitted a still image from a U.S. government system to AARO showing an unidentified dark circular object near the center of a crosshair reticle, with date and location withheld.

title: FBI Photo A5
object_description: Dark, circular object observed in a monochrome image with a dense speckled background and central crosshair reticle; located in the bottom quadrant slightly right of center; no size, speed, or behavioral data provided.
summary_one_line: The FBI submitted a still image from a U.S. government system to AARO showing an unidentified dark circular object captured in a monochrome sensor image, with date and location undisclosed.

title: FBI Photo A6
object_description: Dark, circular object centered on a crosshair reticle in a monochrome image with a lightly textured background; no further behavioral or size details provided.
summary_one_line: FBI submitted a still image to AARO showing an unidentified dark circular object centered on a crosshair reticle, captured by a U.S. government system in late 2025 at an undisclosed location.

title: FBI Photo A7
object_description: Light-colored, circular object with a bright specular highlight, visible in a monochrome image just below the center of a crosshair reticle against a smooth, grainy background.
summary_one_line: FBI submitted a redacted still image to AARO showing an unidentified circular object with a bright specular highlight captured by a U.S. government system in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo A8
object_description: Small, dark, irregular-shaped object visible just below and to the right of a central crosshair reticle in a monochrome image with faint swirling background patterns.
summary_one_line: FBI submitted a still image from a U.S. government system to AARO showing an unidentified small, dark, irregular object that operators could not positively identify.

title: FBI Photo B1
object_description: Small, dark, circular object visible in the upper right quadrant of a monochrome, grainy military sensor image; no additional behavioral or size details available.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured a small, dark, circular unidentified object over the Western United States in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo B10
object_description: Small, dark, circular object visible in the top-right quadrant of a central crosshair reticle in a monochrome, grainy image; background shows an indistinct mountain range.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured a small, dark, circular UAP against a mountain range backdrop in the western United States in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo B11
object_description: Small, dark, circular object visible in the upper right quadrant of a monochrome, grainy military sensor image; background shows an indistinct mountain range; object could not be positively identified by the operator.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured a small, dark circular object over an apparent mountain range in the western United States in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo B12
object_description: Small, dark, circular object visible in the upper right quadrant of a monochrome, grainy military image; no motion or behavior data available due to still-image format.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured a small, dark, circular unidentified object over a mountainous backdrop in the western United States in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo B13
object_description: Two small, dark, elongated objects visible near the center-bottom-right quadrant of a monochrome, grainy still image; no further shape, color gradation, or behavioral details available due to image quality and redactions.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured two small, dark, elongated unidentified objects in late 2025 over the Western United States, with the operator unable to positively identify them.

title: FBI Photo B14
object_description: Two small, dark, circular objects visible near the center of a grainy monochrome frame captured by a U.S. military imaging system; no additional shape, size, or behavioral details available due to redactions.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured two small, dark, circular unidentified objects that operators could not positively identify.

title: FBI Photo B15
object_description: Two small, dark, circular objects visible near the center-upper-right quadrant of a monochrome, grainy still image; no additional behavioral or size details available due to redactions and absence of a mission report.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured two small, dark, circular unidentified objects that operators could not positively identify, with the image date noted as inaccurate due to an unconfigured system clock.

title: FBI Photo B16
object_description: Two dark, irregular-shaped objects visible just right of center in the upper right quadrant of a grainy monochrome image; objects could not be positively identified by the operator.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured two dark, irregular-shaped unidentified objects in the Western United States in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo B17
object_description: Two small, dark, circular objects visible near the center of a monochrome, grainy image captured by a U.S. military system; no additional behavioral or size details provided.
summary_one_line: FBI submitted a still image from a U.S. military system to AARO showing two small, dark, circular unidentified objects that operators could not positively identify.

title: FBI Photo B18
object_description: Two small, dark, elongated objects visible near the center-lower-left of a grainy monochromatic frame; no further shape, color gradation, or behavioral detail available due to image quality and redactions.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured two small, dark, elongated unidentified objects that operators could not positively identify.

title: FBI Photo B19
object_description: A small cluster of dark pixels forming an unidentified object, visible at the center of a crosshair reticle in a grainy monochrome image; shape and behavior indeterminate due to image quality and redactions.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured an unidentified object at the center of a crosshair reticle over the Western United States in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo B2
object_description: Small, dark, circular object visible in the upper right quadrant of a monochrome, grainy image captured through a military optical system with a central crosshair reticle; background shows an indistinct mountain range or cloud formation.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured an unidentified small, dark, circular object over the western United States in late 2025, which the operator could not positively identify.

title: FBI Photo B20
object_description: One to two small, dark objects visible just above and to the right of a central crosshair reticle in a grainy monochrome image; no shape or behavioral details discernible beyond their dark appearance against the image background.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured one to two small, dark, unidentified objects above a targeting reticle over the Western United States in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo B21
object_description: One to two small, dark objects visible just above and to the right of the center of a crosshair reticle in a grainy monochrome image; no shape, size, or behavioral details determinable from the redacted still image.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured one or two small, dark, unidentified objects that the operator could not positively identify, reported from the Western United States in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo B22
object_description: Two small, dark, elongated objects visible near the center of a monochrome, grainy image in the upper right quadrant; no further detail on behavior or speed available.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured two small, dark, elongated unidentified objects that operators could not positively identify.

title: FBI Photo B23
object_description: Single dark, elongated object visible near the edge of a crosshair reticle in a grainy monochrome image; no confirmed shape, color variation, or behavioral data available due to redactions and absence of accompanying mission report.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured a single dark, elongated unidentified object near the reticle center, with the operator unable to positively identify it.

title: FBI Photo B24
object_description: A single dark, irregular-shaped object appearing just above the center of a crosshair reticle in a grainy monochrome image captured by a U.S. military system.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured an unidentified dark, irregular-shaped object that operators could not positively identify, reported to AARO in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo B3
object_description: Small, dark, circular object visible just right of center in a monochrome, grainy image; no observed behavior due to still-image format; background shows an indistinct mountain range or cloud formation.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured a small, dark, circular UAP to the right of a crosshair reticle over what appears to be a mountain range in the western United States in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo B4
object_description: Small, dark, circular object visible in the center-right quadrant of a monochrome, grainy image captured by a U.S. military system; no further detail on behavior or size available due to redactions and lack of accompanying mission report.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured a small, dark, circular UAP over the Western United States in late 2025, with the operator unable to positively identify the object.

title: FBI Photo B5
object_description: No distinct object clearly visible; the monochrome, grainy image shows a central crosshair reticle with an indistinct background formation possibly resembling a mountain range; original imagery was redacted prior to submission.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image captured by a U.S. military system in late 2025 shows an unidentified aerial phenomenon over what appears to be a mountain range in the western United States, with the operator unable to positively identify the object.

title: FBI Photo B6
object_description: A dark, structured object with an appendage on its left side visible near the top of a crosshair reticle, and a second smaller dark circular object in the bottom right quadrant; imaged in monochrome with grainy texture.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured an unidentified structured object with an appendage and a smaller circular object, with the operator unable to positively identify either.

title: FBI Photo B7
object_description: Two dark objects visible in a monochrome, grainy military sensor image: a larger object consistent in appearance with a helicopter in the upper right quadrant, and a second smaller dark circular object below the central crosshair reticle; background is indistinct.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured two unidentified dark objects, one helicopter-like and one circular, in the Western United States in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo B8
object_description: Small, dark, circular object visible just right of center in the top right quadrant of a monochrome, grainy image captured through a military optical system; no propulsion, wings, or other identifying features discernible.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured a small, dark, circular unidentified object near the center of a targeting reticle against a mountainous background in the western United States in late 2025.

title: FBI Photo B9
object_description: Small, dark, circular object appearing in a monochrome, grainy still image, positioned just below and to the left of a central crosshair reticle, with an indistinct mountain range visible in the background.
summary_one_line: An FBI-submitted still image from a U.S. military system captured a small, dark, circular UAP near a mountain range in the western United States in late 2025, with the operator unable to positively identify the object.

title: NASA-UAP-D1, Apollo 12 Transcript, 1969
object_description: Particles and flashes of light observed 'sailing off in space' via the Alignment Optical Telescope, and floating debris illuminated by the lunar module's onboard tracking light; phenomena were characterized as 'escaping the Moon.'
summary_one_line: Apollo 12 astronauts reported observing unexplained particles, flashes of light, and floating debris near the lunar module during the November 1969 mission to the Moon.

title: NASA-UAP-D2, Apollo 17 Transcript, 1972
object_description: Bright tumbling particles or fragments drifting near spacecraft; intense flashing lights described as bright as a train headlight; rotating flashing objects assessed as physical rather than optical; brief flash observed on the lunar surface north of Grimaldi crater
summary_one_line: Apollo 17 astronauts reported multiple unidentified phenomena including bright tumbling particles, intense flashing lights, and a flash on the lunar surface during their December 1972 mission.

title: NASA-UAP-D4, Apollo 11 Technical Crew Debriefing, 1969
object_description: A sizeable unidentified object observed approximately one day from the Moon, viewed through a monocular; separate observations included small periodic light flashes inside the darkened cabin spaced minutes apart, and a fairly bright light source on the return trip tentatively identified as a laser.
summary_one_line: Apollo 11 astronauts reported three anomalous observations during the lunar mission: an unidentified object near the Moon, unexplained light flashes inside the cabin, and a bright light on the return trip possibly attributed to a laser.

title: NASA-UAP-D5, Apollo 17 Crew Debriefing for Science, 1973
object_description: Anomalous ultraviolet spectrum consistent with hot star emissions detected at high galactic latitudes, with no known hot stars within the field of view; the signal exceeded expected dark current levels and displayed characteristics not fully explained by interstellar dust reflection.
summary_one_line: Apollo 17 ultraviolet experiment detected an unexpected spectrum resembling hot stars where no hot stars were present, possibly indicating extragalactic radiation.

title: NASA-UAP-D6, Apollo 17 Technical Crew Debriefing, 1973
object_description: Intermittent light flashes seen by dark-adapted astronaut, perceived continuously during flight in darkness and possibly on the lunar surface; not externally visible objects but subjective luminous phenomena, likely cosmic ray interactions with the retina or spacecraft optics.
summary_one_line: Apollo 17 astronaut Harrison Schmitt reported continuous light flashes observed during dark-adapted periods throughout the lunar mission, including one suspected flash on the lunar surface.

title: NASA-UAP-D7, Skylab Techincal Crew Debriefing 1973
object_description: Bright reddish object resembling a large red star, brighter than Jupiter, observed in a similar orbital trajectory and tracked for 5–10 minutes; separate observations of flashing lights with definite motion relative to the station, and internal light flashes perceived by crew with eyes closed.
summary_one_line: Skylab astronauts from three successive crews reported unexplained light flashes, a bright reddish object tracked for 5–10 minutes in similar orbit, and lights with definite relative motion outside the station between 1973 and 1974.

title: NASA-UAP-VM1, Apollo 12, 1969
object_description: Unidentified phenomenon visible above the lunar horizon, slightly right of center in the frame; specific shape, color, and behavior not described beyond a highlighted area of interest in the archival image.
summary_one_line: An archival Apollo 12 photograph taken from the lunar surface in 1969 contains a highlighted unidentified phenomenon visible above the horizon to the right of the frame's vertical axis.

title: NASA-UAP-VM2, Apollo 12, 1969
object_description: Two unidentified phenomena visible in distinct areas (Area 1 and Area 2) above the lunar horizon, slightly right of center frame; exact shape, color, and behavior not specified beyond their anomalous appearance in the photograph.
summary_one_line: An Apollo 12 archival photograph taken from the lunar surface shows two highlighted areas above the horizon containing unidentified phenomena.

title: NASA-UAP-VM3, Apollo 12, 1969
object_description: Unidentified phenomenon appearing as a highlighted area of interest near the right edge of the photographic frame, above the lunar horizon; specific shape, color, and behavior not described beyond its anomalous presence in the image.
summary_one_line: An archival Apollo 12 photograph taken from the lunar surface shows an unidentified phenomenon visible near the right edge of the frame above the horizon.

title: NASA-UAP-VM4, Apollo 12, 1969
object_description: Unidentified phenomenon visible above the lunar horizon in a still photograph; a highlighted area of interest was marked in the modified image, but no specific shape, color, or behavior is described beyond its anomalous presence in the frame.
summary_one_line: An archival Apollo 12 photograph taken from the lunar surface in 1969 shows an unidentified phenomenon visible above the horizon, slightly left of center frame.

title: NASA-UAP-VM5, Apollo 12, 1969
object_description: Five distinct unidentified phenomena visible above the lunar horizon, labeled Area 1 through Area 5; specific shapes, colors, or behaviors are not described beyond their presence in the image.
summary_one_line: An archival Apollo 12 photograph taken from the lunar surface shows five highlighted areas above the horizon containing unidentified phenomena.

title: NASA-UAP-VM6, Apollo 17, 1972
object_description: Three small dots arranged in a triangular formation, visible in the lower right quadrant of the lunar sky upon magnification of the Apollo 17 mission photograph.
summary_one_line: An Apollo 17 photograph from December 1972 shows three unidentified dots in a triangular formation in the lunar sky, with preliminary US government analysis suggesting a possible physical object.

title: State Department UAP Cable 1, Papua New Guinea, January 28, 1985
object_description: High-altitude, high-speed aerial objects exhibiting lights, contrails, and audible noise, moving fast enough to be tracked on aircraft radar; described as flying south to north at high altitude and high speed.
summary_one_line: On January 24, 1985, residents of Papua New Guinea reported high-altitude, high-speed unidentified aerial objects with lights, contrails, and noise, corroborated by an Air Niugini pilot's radar detection, prompting a U.S. Embassy inquiry to INDOPACOM.

title: State Department UAP Cable 2, Kazakhstan, January 31, 1994
object_description: Bright light of enormous intensity, resembling a bullet in flight; approached rapidly from the east at a much higher altitude than the aircraft; performed 90-degree turns, corkscrews, and high-speed circular maneuvers; produced contrails estimated at 100,000 feet altitude, too high for ordinary aircraft.
summary_one_line: A Tajik pilot and three American citizens aboard a 747 at 41,000 feet over Kazakhstan observed a bullet-shaped UAP emitting intense light, performing 90-degree turns, corkscrews, and high-speed circular maneuvers with contrails estimated at 100,000 feet altitude.

title: State Department UAP Cable 3, Tbilisi, Georgia, October 30, 2001
object_description: No physical object described; Russia suggested UFOs as an explanation to deny responsibility for alleged airspace violations and bombings over the Kodori Gorge.
summary_one_line: Russian aircraft allegedly violated Georgian airspace and bombed the Kodori Gorge on October 28-29, 2001, with Russia deflecting blame by suggesting the incidents could have been caused by UFOs.

title: State Department UAP Cable 4, Ashgabat, Turkmenistan, November 5, 2004
summary_one_line: A US State Department cable from Ashgabat, Turkmenistan notes the UFOlogists of Turkmenistan organization has earned a positive reputation as a civil society partner, with the author bemused that virtually all Turkmen citizens claim interest in UFOs.

title: State Department UAP Cable 5, Mexico, September 16, 2003
object_description: Fast-moving flying objects recorded on video during Mexican pilot flight encounters; alleged alien corpses also presented as evidence.
summary_one_line: On September 12, 2003, the Mexican Congress heard expert testimony on UAPs, including alleged alien corpses and videos of fast-moving objects encountered by Mexican pilots, as part of a debate on an Aerial Space Protection Law.

title: USPER Statement about UAP Sighting
object_description: Spherical 'super-hot' orb hovering over the ground, capable of traveling 20+ miles at extreme speed; accompanied by a swarm of lights moving in all directions and four to five additional orbs that repeatedly flared up and down over a thirty-minute period.
summary_one_line: A senior US intelligence official and other federal and state personnel observed multiple 'super-hot' orbs and a swarm of lights at a US military facility, with one orb traveling over 20 miles at speeds exceeding helicopter pursuit.

title: FBI September 2023 Sighting - Composite Sketch
object_description: Ellipsoid bronze metallic object approximately 130–195 feet in length, observed materializing out of a bright light in the sky and disappearing instantaneously; documented via FBI Lab composite sketch overlaid on actual site photo.
summary_one_line: FBI-documented eyewitness reports from September 2023 describe an ellipsoid bronze metallic object materializing from a bright light, measuring 130–195 feet in length, before vanishing instantaneously.

title: FBI September 2023 Sighting - Serial 3
object_description: Metallic bronze in color; no further shape or behavioral details provided due to redaction.
summary_one_line: A US citizen reported a first-hand UAP encounter at a US test site, describing a metallic bronze-colored object, as documented in an FBI 302 interview in September 2023.

title: FBI September 2023 Sighting - Serial 4
object_description: Metallic/gray in color; no further shape or behavioral details provided due to redaction.
summary_one_line: A US citizen reported a first-hand UAP encounter at a US test site, describing a metallic/gray object, as recorded in an FBI 302 interview in September 2023.

title: FBI September 2023 Sighting - Serial 5
location_name: United States (US test site, location undisclosed)
object_description: Bright light observed over the horizon; no further shape or size details provided due to redaction.
summary_one_line: A US citizen reported observing a bright light over the horizon during a first-hand UAP encounter at a US test site in September 2023.

title: Western US Event
object_description: Multiple distinct phenomena reported: luminous orbs observed launching smaller orbs at a distance; a large stationary glowing orb observed at close range; a large phenomenon observed near the ground during pursuit; and a large seemingly transparent object described as akin to a 'translucent kite'.
summary_one_line: Seven federal government employees separately reported observing multiple unidentified aerial phenomena including glowing orbs, orbs launching other orbs, and a translucent kite-like object across the western United States over two days in 2023.
